Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 5:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast North West England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Turning more unsettled through the week. Today: A bright start with some good spells of sunshine. However, skies will cloud over through the morning, perhaps with a few showers for a time. Warm bright spells developing again in the afternoon. Maximum temperature 19 °C. Tonight: Clouding over again this evening with a few light showers possible overnight. Staying mild under the blanket of cloud with light winds. Minimum temperature 14 °C. Tuesday: Cloudy on Tuesday with some light showers at first. A few brighter spells possible for a time, before a spell of heavy rain moves in from the south later. Maximum temperature 23 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Unsettled at times over the coming days with showers and spells of rain. However, some drier, warmer and brighter interludes are possible too. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times.
